seoTitle: Cherryville vs Chilpenunda — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Cherryville and Chilpenunda. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cherryville and Chilpenunda.
heading: Cherryville vs Chilpenunda — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cherryville, South Australia and Chilpenunda, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cherryville has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Chilpenunda (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cherryville or Chilpenunda safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cherryville has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Chilpenunda scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cherryville scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Chilpenunda scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cherryville or Chilpenunda?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cherryville has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.62% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
